questions.original_question: 2024年9月27日LTIL和Geely UK Limited之间签署的股权转让协议，请简述协议内容
questions.answer:
关于2024年9月27日路特斯科技创新有限公司（LTIL）与吉利英国有限公司签署的股权转让协议的具体内容，目前没有公开的详细信息。根据现有资料，该协议涉及LTIL向吉利英国有限公司出售其在德国子公司Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH的80%股权，交易金额为1088万欧元。协议签署后，交易已同时完成。

以上信息基于2024年10月01日之前的可用知识。
rewritten_question: What is the content of the equity transfer agreement signed between LTIL and Geely UK Limited on September 27, 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK Pursuant to the Distribution Agreement entered into by and between Lotus Cars Limited, the entity carrying out Lotus UK’s sportscar manufacturing operations, and LTIL, Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where LTIL will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Cars Limited. The Distribution Agreement also provides that each year Lotus Technology and Lotus UK will prepare business plans and annual targets taking into account historical sales figures, forecast demand, national, regional, and local trends, and Lotus UK’s production capacity for the vehicles. Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the content of the equity transfer agreement signed between LTIL and Geely UK Limited on September 27, 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Under the terms of the agreement, Lotus Technology received a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and non-sublicensable license for the electric automotive chassis and autonomous driving technology platform, referred to as the “Geely License,” with a consideration of US$288.9 million. The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain of Lotus Technology's R&D projects. Lotus Technology concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. In November 2021, Lotus Technology obtained the trademark licenses from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, a subsidiary of Lotus Technology, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at a consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the content of the equity transfer agreement signed between LTIL and Geely UK Limited on September 27, 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Concurrent with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025, to June 30, 2025, and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the content of the equity transfer agreement signed between LTIL and Geely UK Limited on September 27, 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.

rag_info.sub_query: What is the content of the equity transfer agreement signed between LTIL and Geely UK Limited on September 27, 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Pursuant to the Distribution Agreement entered into by and between Lotus Cars Limited, the entity carrying out Lotus UK’s sportscar manufacturing operations, and Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL), our subsidiary, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the United States, where LTIL will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Cars Limited. The Distribution Agreement also provides that each year Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Lotus UK will prepare business plans and annual targets taking into account historical sales figures, forecast demand, national, regional, and local trends, and Lotus UK’s production capacity for the vehicles. Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology Innovative Limited. As of September 30, 2023, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited had 206 stores in its global distribution network. In addition to the Eletre and future BEV models, major vehicle models under the Distribution Agreement include the Evija (BEV sports car) and the Emira (ICE sports car), both developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, and another BEV sports car expected to be launched by Lotus UK in 2025. Emira (ICE sports car) Launched in 2021 with delivery in 2022, the Emira is built on a new Lotus sports car architecture. The Emira uses the pioneering Lotus bonded extruded aluminum chassis technology.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the content of the equity transfer agreement signed between LTIL and Geely UK Limited on September 27, 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: Each of the put options granted to Geely and Etika may only be exercised during the period from April 1 to June 30, 2025, and is contingent upon satisfaction of the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries exceeds 5,000 in 2024. The put options granted to Geely and Etika can be exercised independently from each other, and the exercise of each put option is not conditioned upon the exercise of the other.

rag_info.chunk_content: On January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology issued put options to each of Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited (“Geely HK”) and Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd (“Etika”) at nil consideration. Pursuant to the put option agreements, each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of LGIL, during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price, i.e. 1.15 multiplied by the revenue of LGIL for the year ending December 31, 2024 plus the cash and cash equivalents of LGIL as of December 31, 2024, and minus the outstanding amount of indebtedness of LGIL as of December 31, 2024 (the “exercise price”), if the total number of vehicles sold by LGIL in 2024 exceeds 5,000 (the “exercise condition”). Geely HK and Etika are entitled to exercise the put option on an individual standalone basis. If

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (the “LTIL”), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., recently entered into a share sale and transfer agreement (the “Share Transfer Agreement”) with Geely UK Limited (“Geely”), pursuant to which LTIL agrees to sell, and Geely agrees to purchase, 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (“LTIC”), a company incorporated in Germany and a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL, for an aggregate principal amount of EUR 10,880,000 (the “Transaction”). The Transaction closed simultaneously with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. Upon completion of the Transaction, Geely beneficially owns 80% of LTIC’s share capital. A copy of the Share Transfer Agreement is included in this current report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the foregoing description of the Share Transfer Agreement is qualified in its entirety by reference thereto. Share Sale and Transfer Agreement, dated September 27, 2024, by and between Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Geely UK Limited.

metadata.title_summary:
title: LTIC Share Transfer Agreement 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the LTIC Share Transfer Agreement**

Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology Inc., has executed a Share Transfer Agreement with Geely UK Limited (Geely) for the sale of 800,000 shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH (LTIC), a German company that is also a wholly-owned subsidiary of LTIL. The total transaction amount for this share transfer is EUR 10,880,000. 

The transaction was finalized on September 27, 2024, coinciding with the signing of the Share Transfer Agreement. As a result of this transaction, Geely now possesses 80% of LTIC's share capital. The details of the Share Transfer Agreement are documented in this report on Form 6-K as Exhibit 10.1, and the description provided here is subject to the complete terms outlined in that exhibit.

This agreement marks a significant strategic move for both LTIL and Geely, reflecting Geely's increased stake in LTIC and reinforcing its position in the market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025 and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC finalized a put option agreement with Geely and Etika, complementing the Original Merger Agreement. This arrangement allows Geely and Etika to compel LTC to buy their equity stakes in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The purchase price will be based on LGIL's total revenue for 2024, adjusted for net debt, and the transaction can take place between April 1, 2025, and June 30, 2025, provided specific pre-agreed conditions are met.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrent with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a put option agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025, to June 30, 2025, and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into put option agreements with Geely and Etika as part of the Original Merger Agreement. These agreements provide Geely and Etika with the right to compel LTC to purchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, which is the parent company of Lotus UK. The purchase price for these equity interests will be determined based on the total revenue of LGIL for the fiscal year 2024, with adjustments made to exclude net debt. The options can be exercised within a specified timeframe, from April 1, 2025, to June 30, 2025, and their execution is contingent upon the fulfillment of certain pre-agreed conditions. This arrangement is designed to ensure that both Geely and Etika have a clear exit strategy while aligning their interests with LTC's performance in the upcoming fiscal year.

metadata.title_summary:
title: CONVERTIBLE NOTE PURCHASE AGREEMENT 
summary: Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited, a subsidiary of Geely Automobile Holdings, is dedicated to expanding the company's global footprint in the automotive sector. It manages international investments, partnerships, and promotes Geely's vehicles and technologies worldwide, emphasizing innovation, sustainability, and customer experience. Additionally, a Convertible Note Purchase Agreement dated June 24, 2024, has been established between Lotus Technology Inc. and Geely International, detailing the investment terms for convertible notes issued by Lotus.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC established a put option agreement with Geely HK and Etika in conjunction with the Original Merger Agreement. This arrangement allows Geely HK and Etika to require LTC to repurchase their equity stakes in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The buyback price will be based on LGIL's total revenue for 2024, adjusted for net debt, and the transaction is set to take place between April 1 and June 30, 2025, contingent upon meeting specific pre-agreed conditions.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology entered into put option agreements with each of Geely HK and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of LGIL, during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price, i.e. 1.15 multiplied by the revenue of LGIL for the year ending December 31, 2024 plus the cash and cash equivalents of LGIL as of December 31, 2024, and minus the outstanding amount of indebtedness of LGIL as of December 31, 2024, at a future date and upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions, i.e. total number of vehicles sold by LGIL in 2024 exceeds 5,000. Upon exercise by each of Geely HK and Etika of the put option, LGIL agrees to take any and all actions to distribute Lotus Technology’s shares held by LGIL on the date of the put exercise note to each of Geely HK and Etika such that Geely HK and Etika each shall receive the pro rata number of Lotus Technology’s shares held by LGIL concurrently with the completion of the exercise of the put option.

metadata.title_summary:
title: (iii)  Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, the Company established put option agreements with Geely HK and Etika, allowing them to compel the Company to buy their equity stakes in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of LGIL. This option can be exercised between April 1 and June 30, 2025, at a price determined by 1.15 times LGIL's revenue for the year ending December 31, 2024, plus its cash and cash equivalents, minus any outstanding debt. The option is contingent upon LGIL selling over 5,000 vehicles in 2024. If exercised, LGIL will distribute the Company's shares it holds to Geely HK and Etika in proportion to their respective interests at the time of the option's execution.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 13.   PUT OPTION LIABILITIES 
summary: The "Put Option Liabilities Reconciliation" provides an overview of the changes in put option liabilities from December 31, 2022, to June 30, 2023. It highlights the issuance of put options, their fair value fluctuations, and the final balance as of June 30, 2023, all in US dollars. 

The reconciliation is based on put option agreements established on January 31, 2023, with Geely HK and Etika, which grant these entities the right to sell their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd back to the Company, contingent on LGIL's sales performance in 2024. 

Key details include an initial balance of zero for put option liabilities at the end of 2022, the issuance of options valued at US$9,376 on January 31, 2023, and a subsequent decrease in fair values amounting to US$(3,307) by June 30, 2023. This resulted in a final balance of US$6,069 in put option liabilities. 

The options, granted at no cost, can be exercised between April 1, 2025, and June 30, 2025, provided LGIL sells over 5,000 vehicles in 2024. The exercise price is determined by a formula based on LGIL's revenue, cash reserves, and outstanding debts as of December 31, 2024. Both Geely HK and Etika have the ability to exercise their options independently.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Under the terms of the agreement, Lotus Technology received a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and non-sublicensable license for the electric automotive chassis and autonomous driving technology platform, referred to as the “Geely License,” with a consideration of US$288.9 million. The Geely License is contractually restricted to be used in certain of Lotus Technology's R&D projects. Lotus Technology concludes that the Geely License has no alternative future use; therefore, the cost of the license has been expensed as research and development expenses in the consolidated and combined statement of comprehensive loss for the year ended December 31, 2021. In November 2021, Lotus Technology obtained the trademark licenses from Group Lotus Limited, a related party controlled by Geely Holding, by issuing 47,995,443 ordinary shares to LGIL, a related party controlled by Geely Holding. On December 2, 2021, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited, a subsidiary of Lotus Technology, entered into an equity transfer agreement, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Innovative Limited agreed to acquire 100% equity interest in Lotus Tech Innovation Centre GmbH from a related party, Geely UK Limited, at a consideration of US$15.5 million, which was settled in June 2022.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Related Party Transactions 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Related Party Transactions

The "Related Party Transactions" chapter outlines the company's extensive financial interactions with related parties over the years, particularly focusing on the sale of sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and consulting services. 

#### Revenue from Related Party Transactions
- **Service Revenue**: The company generated revenue from services provided to related parties amounting to:
  - **2023**: US$12.6 million
  - **2022**: US$8.3 million
  - **2021**: US$3.3 million
- **Product Sales**: Sales of products to related parties were:
  - **2023**: US$3.4 million
  - **2022**: US$23 thousand
  - **2021**: None

#### Accounts Receivable and Payments
- As of December 31, 2023, accounts receivable from related parties totaled **US$22.4 million**, up from **US$8.5 million** in 2022.
- The company made payments on behalf of related parties totaling:
  - **2023**: US$4.0 million
  - **2022**: US$244 thousand
  - **2021**: None
- Related parties collected **US$3.7 million** in 2023.

#### Loans and Prepayments
- Unsecured short-term loans to related parties were:
  - **2023**: US$1.2 million
  - **2022**: US$2.3 million
- Interest income from these loans was recognized at:
  - **2023**: US$122 thousand
  - **2022**: US$24 thousand
- Prepayments for vehicle purchases to related parties amounted to:
  - **2023**: US$21.4 million
  - **2022**: US$6.1 million
- An expected credit loss provision for these prepayments increased from **US$12 thousand** on January 1, 2023, to **US$23 thousand** by December 31, 2023.

#### Purchases from Related Parties
- Total purchases from related parties were significant, amounting to:
  - **2023**: US$852.6 million (with **US$549.9 million** recognized as cost of goods sold)
  - **2022**: US$44.3 million
  - **2021**: US$14.3 million
- Research and development expenses related to these purchases were:
  - **2023**: US$134.7 million
  - **2022**: US$167.0 million
  - **2021**: US$47.4 million
- Equipment and software purchases from related parties totaled **US$11.2 million** in 2023 and **US$42.8 million** in 2022.
- The company incurred short-term lease costs of **US$765 thousand** in 2023.

#### Agreements and Investments
- In 2023, the company established a distribution agreement with Lotus Cars Limited, recording a payable of **US$23.0 million** for distribution rights.
- The company had previously entered into a license agreement for the Geely License, expensing **US$288.9 million** as R&D costs in 2021.
- Additionally, equity interests were acquired in related entities, including a **US$15.5 million** transaction with Geely UK Limited completed in June 2022.
- The fair value of a convertible note purchased from ECARX Holding Inc. was **US$3.3 million** as of December 31, 2023.

Overall, the transactions with related parties reflect significant financial interactions that have notably impacted both revenues and expenses across multiple years, underscoring the importance of these relationships in the company's financial landscape.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into put option agreements with each of Geely HK and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely HK and Etika will have an option to require Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) to purchase the equity interests held by Geely HK and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the immediate parent of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL), during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025, at a pre-agreed price, i.e. 1.15 multiplied by the revenue of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL) for the year ending December 31, 2024 plus the cash and cash equivalents of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL) as of December 31, 2024, and minus the outstanding amount of indebtedness of Lotus Group International Limited (LGIL) as of December 31, 2024, at a future date and upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions. During March to April 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into convertible note purchase agreements with certain investors. One of the investors of Pre-IPO Notes, a related party controlled by the controlling shareholder of Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC), agreed to subscribe to a convertible note for a cash consideration of US$9 million, which has not been issued as of September 30, 2023. On November 6, 2023, the subscription amount by the related party controlled by the controlling shareholder of Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) changed from US$9 million to US$9.5 million, which has not been issued as of the issuance of the unaudited condensed financial statements.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Other Related Party Transactions 
summary: Lotus Tech has been actively involved in the sale of battery electric vehicles (BEVs), auto parts, and providing research and development (R&D) and consulting services, particularly to related parties. In the first half of 2023, the company reported a significant increase in service revenue, reaching $5.0 million, up from $1.9 million in the same period of 2022. Product sales also saw growth, with $807,000 in BEVs and auto parts sold to related parties in early 2023, compared to no sales in the previous year. By mid-2023, accounts receivable from related parties stood at $13.6 million, reflecting a consistent upward trend from previous years.

Lotus Tech's financial activities also include substantial transactions related to licensing agreements, notably with Zhejiang Liankong, a subsidiary of Geely Holding. Initially, the licensing cost was set at approximately $888.2 million but was later reduced to around $306.3 million. This license, which is limited to specific R&D projects, has been recorded as an expense, indicating no alternative future use. Additionally, the company has engaged in various financial arrangements with related parties, including R&D service payments, salary and expense reimbursements, and unsecured loans, which have contributed to a growing receivable balance.

The company has also entered into significant lease agreements for office and parking spaces, resulting in the recognition of right-of-use assets. In terms of purchases from related parties, Lotus Tech reported a dramatic increase to $264.4 million in the first half of 2023, compared to $10.1 million in the same period of 2022. This surge included substantial costs of goods sold and inventory levels.

Lotus Tech's financial obligations to related parties have increased over time, with amounts owed for various services and products rising significantly. The company has also engaged in equity transactions, including the acquisition of subsidiaries and investments in convertible notes, further expanding its financial landscape. Recent agreements, such as the distribution rights with Lotus Cars Limited and the trademark license agreements, highlight Lotus Tech's strategic partnerships and growth initiatives.

Overall, Lotus Tech's financial activities reflect a robust engagement with related parties, characterized by increasing revenues, significant investments in R&D, and a growing portfolio of agreements that position the company for future growth in the electric vehicle market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into a Put Option Agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on the total revenue of Lotus Group International Limited for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt, at a future date during the period from April 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025 and contingent upon satisfaction of certain pre-agreed conditions. See “Agreements Entered Into in Connection with the Business Combination    —     Put Option Agreements.” Concurrently with the execution of the Original Merger Agreement, on January 31, 2023, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) entered into the Put Option Agreement with each of Geely and Etika, pursuant to which each of Geely and Etika is granted the right, but not the obligation, to require LTC to purchase all of the equity interests held by each of Geely and Etika in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK, at a pre-agreed price which will be calculated based on Lotus Group International Limited’s total revenue for the year of 2024 adjusted to exclude net debt.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into a Put Option Agreement with Geely and Etika in conjunction with the Original Merger Agreement. This agreement allows Geely and Etika to require LTC to repurchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The repurchase price will be determined based on the total revenue of Lotus Group International Limited for the year 2024, adjusted for net debt. The put options can be exercised between April 1 and June 30, 2025, contingent upon Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries selling over 5,000 vehicles in 2024.

Title: Director and Chief Financial Officer THE SYMBOL “[REDACTED]” DENOTES PLACES WHERE CERTAIN IDENTIFIED INFORMATION HAS BEEN EXCLUDED FROM THE EXHIBIT BECAUSE IT IS BOTH (I) NOT MATERIAL, AND (II) IS THE TYPE THAT LOTUS COMPANY TREATS AS PRIVATE OR CONFIDENTIAL. Geely UK Limited Lotus Technology Innovative Limited SHARE SALE AND TRANSFER AGREEMENT relating to the sale and transfer of shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH THIS SHARE SALE AND TRANSFER AGREEMENT (the “Agreement”) is made on September 27, 2024 (the “Signing Date”)

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Inc. 
summary: On September 27, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. executed a Share Sale and Transfer Agreement concerning the sale and transfer of shares in Lotus Tech Innovation Center GmbH. This agreement involves Geely UK Limited and Lotus Technology Innovative Limited. The document was signed by Alexious Kuen Long Lee, who serves as the Director and Chief Financial Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. Certain details within the agreement have been redacted, as they are considered non-material and treated as private or confidential by the company, indicated by the symbol “[REDACTED].” This transaction reflects Lotus Technology Inc.'s strategic initiatives and partnerships aimed at enhancing its operational capabilities and market presence.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Each of the put options granted to Geely and Etika may only be exercised during the period from April 1 to June 30, 2025, and is contingent upon satisfaction of the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries exceeds 5,000 in 2024. The put options granted to Geely and Etika can be exercised independently from each other, and the exercise of each put option is not conditioned upon the exercise of the other.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Put Option Agreements 
summary: On January 31, 2023, LTC entered into a Put Option Agreement with Geely and Etika in conjunction with the Original Merger Agreement. This agreement allows Geely and Etika to require LTC to repurchase their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd, the parent company of Lotus UK. The repurchase price will be determined based on the total revenue of Lotus Group International Limited for the year 2024, adjusted for net debt. The put options can be exercised between April 1 and June 30, 2025, contingent upon Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries selling over 5,000 vehicles in 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: EXHIBIT INDEX 
summary: On June 24, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. finalized a Convertible Note Purchase Agreement with Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited, allowing Geely to invest in Lotus through senior convertible notes. This arrangement grants Geely the option to convert the debt into equity in the future, creating opportunities for mutual growth. In a press release, Lotus Technology emphasized the importance of this investment as a strategic partnership that is poised to bolster its capabilities and enhance its market position in the industry.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK Pursuant to the Distribution Agreement entered into by and between Lotus Cars Limited, the entity carrying out Lotus UK’s sportscar manufacturing operations, and LTIL, Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where LTIL will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Cars Limited. The Distribution Agreement also provides that each year Lotus Technology and Lotus UK will prepare business plans and annual targets taking into account historical sales figures, forecast demand, national, regional, and local trends, and Lotus UK’s production capacity for the vehicles. Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Global Commercial Platform 
summary: The Global Commercial Platform for Lotus UK vehicles is designed to enhance the sales and distribution experience, featuring a network of 215 stores as of December 31, 2023. This platform emphasizes a luxurious and future-proof customer experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, catering to models such as the Eletre, Emeya, and upcoming Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs). Key components of the platform include a virtual showroom, an inquiry and customization platform, a reservation system for test drives, and comprehensive aftersales services. Customers are offered extensive vehicle personalization options and digital payment methods to streamline their purchasing experience.

Under a Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus Cars Limited, Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL) operates as the exclusive global distributor for Lotus vehicles, parts, and tools, excluding the U.S. market. This agreement encompasses branding, marketing, and aftersales services, with LTIL required to develop annual business plans and targets based on historical sales data, demand forecasts, and production capacity. The transition of existing Lotus UK stores and dealers to Lotus Tech is part of this strategic initiative. The major vehicle models included under this agreement are the Eletre, Emeya, future BEVs, as well as sports cars like the Evija and Emira.

Overall, the Global Commercial Platform aims to position Lotus as a leader in the luxury automotive market by leveraging innovative technology and a robust distribution framework to meet evolving customer demands.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the Distribution Agreement entered into by and between Lotus Cars Limited, the entity carrying out Lotus UK’s sportscar manufacturing operations, and Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL), Lotus Technology Innovative Limited is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the United States, where LTIL will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Cars Limited. The Distribution Agreement also provides that each year Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Lotus UK will prepare business plans and annual targets taking into account historical sales figures, forecast demand, national, regional, and local trends, and Lotus UK’s production capacity for the vehicles. Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology Innovative Limited. As of September 30, 2023, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited had 206 stores in its global distribution network. In addition to the Eletre and future BEV models, major vehicle models under the Distribution Agreement include the Evija (BEV sports car) and the Emira (ICE sports car), both developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, and another BEV sports car expected to be launched by Lotus UK in 2025. Emira (ICE sports car) Launched in 2021 with delivery in 2022, the Emira is built on a new Lotus sports car architecture. The Emira uses the pioneering Lotus bonded extruded aluminum chassis technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK 
summary: The Distribution Agreement between Lotus Cars Limited and Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL) establishes LTIL as the exclusive global distributor of Lotus vehicles, parts, and tools, with specific responsibilities for aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations. Excluding the United States, where LTIL acts as the head distributor, the agreement emphasizes collaboration on annual business plans based on sales data and production capacity. As of September 30, 2023, LTIL operates 206 stores worldwide, including existing Lotus UK dealers, and covers major models such as the Eletre, Evija, and Emira, with plans for additional electric vehicle (BEV) models.

The Emira, launched in 2021, features a lightweight aluminum chassis and impressive performance metrics, including a top speed of 290 km/h and a projected annual sales target of 5,000 to 6,000 units starting in 2024. The Evija, unveiled in 2019, is an all-electric hypercar known for its extraordinary power output of around 2,000 horsepower, lightweight construction, and rapid charging capabilities, emphasizing Lotus's commitment to high-performance electric vehicles.

The distribution process for these vehicles involves comprehensive logistics and supply chain management, ensuring efficient delivery and customer satisfaction through effective inventory management and route optimization. LTIL is responsible for creating a distribution network and ensuring compliance with the Distribution Agreement, while also providing aftersales services that enhance customer loyalty and satisfaction. These services include maintenance, repair, and customer support, all aimed at delivering a positive ownership experience for Lotus customers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the Distribution Agreement entered into by and between Lotus Cars Limited, the entity carrying out Lotus UK’s sportscar manufacturing operations, and Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL), our subsidiary, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the United States, where LTIL will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Cars Limited. The Distribution Agreement also provides that each year Lotus Technology Innovative Limited and Lotus UK will prepare business plans and annual targets taking into account historical sales figures, forecast demand, national, regional, and local trends, and Lotus UK’s production capacity for the vehicles. Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology Innovative Limited. As of September 30, 2023, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited had 206 stores in its global distribution network. In addition to the Eletre and future BEV models, major vehicle models under the Distribution Agreement include the Evija (BEV sports car) and the Emira (ICE sports car), both developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, and another BEV sports car expected to be launched by Lotus UK in 2025. Emira (ICE sports car) Launched in 2021 with delivery in 2022, the Emira is built on a new Lotus sports car architecture. The Emira uses the pioneering Lotus bonded extruded aluminum chassis technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK 
summary: The Distribution Agreement between Lotus Cars Limited and its subsidiary, Lotus Technology Innovative Limited (LTIL), establishes LTIL as the exclusive global distributor for Lotus vehicles, parts, and tools, with specific provisions for the U.S. market. This agreement outlines LTIL's responsibilities in aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations, and mandates annual collaboration with Lotus UK to develop business plans and set sales targets based on historical data and forecasts. The transition of existing Lotus UK stores and dealers to Lotus Tech is part of this strategy, which currently includes 206 stores worldwide.

Key vehicle models under this agreement include the Emira, Evija, and upcoming battery electric vehicles (BEVs). The Emira, launched in 2021, features a bonded aluminum chassis, a power output of 298 kW, and an MSRP exceeding $85,000, with projected annual sales of 5,000 to 6,000 units starting in 2024. The Evija, an all-electric hypercar launched in 2020, boasts a remarkable 2,000 horsepower and a top speed of 320 km/h, with an average MSRP of over $2.2 million. It is recognized for its lightweight carbon fiber structure and advanced technology.

The distribution process encompasses logistics from manufacturers to consumers, involving transportation, warehousing, and inventory management, all aimed at enhancing customer satisfaction and market reach. LTIL is responsible for establishing a distribution and aftersales service network, ensuring compliance with standards, and managing sub-distributors. This includes obtaining necessary licenses for vehicle imports while Lotus UK handles vehicle homologation.

Aftersales services play a crucial role in customer satisfaction and brand loyalty, encompassing warranty support, repairs, and maintenance. LTIL is committed to providing a seamless aftersales experience, focusing on customer needs and ensuring vehicle performance through thorough inspections and testing.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK Pursuant to the Distribution Agreement entered into by and between Lotus Cars Limited, the entity carrying out Lotus UK’s sportscar manufacturing operations, and Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL), Lotus Technology International Limited is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where LTIL will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Cars Limited. The Distribution Agreement also provides that each year Lotus Technology International Limited and Lotus UK will prepare business plans and annual targets taking into account historical sales figures, forecast demand, national, regional, and local trends, and Lotus UK’s production capacity for the vehicles. Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology International Limited. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology International Limited had 215 stores in its global distribution network. Major vehicle models under the Distribution Agreement include Eletre, Emeya, and future battery electric vehicle (BEV) models, as well as certain sports car models that were or will be developed and manufactured by Lotus UK such as Evija (BEV sports car) and Emira (internal combustion engine sports car).

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Global Commercial Platform 
summary: As of December 31, 2023, we have successfully developed a Global Commercial Platform for the sales and distribution of Lotus UK vehicles and sports cars, supported by a network of 215 stores worldwide. Our approach to luxury retailing emphasizes exclusivity, personalized service, and high-quality offerings, creating a curated shopping environment that embodies our brand's identity. Customers enjoy exceptional service from knowledgeable staff, along with unique experiences such as private shopping, bespoke products, and exclusive events. We leverage technology to enhance customer engagement through immersive experiences like virtual try-ons and personalized online services.

To ensure a luxurious and future-proof customer experience, we are implementing a digital-first, omni-channel sales model for our Eletre, Emeya, and upcoming battery electric vehicle (BEV) models. This model fosters direct customer relationships and encompasses both in-person and online experiences. Our global sales digital platform features a virtual showroom, tools for inquiries and customization, a reservation system for test drives, and comprehensive aftersales services. Customers can enjoy extensive customization options and utilize our digital payment system.

Additionally, a Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK outlines the terms for distributing their products, including pricing, delivery schedules, and marketing obligations. Under this agreement, LTIL is designated as the exclusive global distributor for Lotus vehicles, parts, and tools, with specific responsibilities for aftersales services and branding. The agreement also requires annual business plans based on sales forecasts and production capacity. As a result, the existing Lotus UK stores and dealers have been integrated into Lotus Tech, solidifying our global distribution network and enhancing our commitment to delivering exceptional luxury experiences.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK Pursuant to the Distribution Agreement entered into by and between Lotus Cars Limited, the entity carrying out Lotus UK’s sportscar manufacturing operations, and Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL), Lotus Technology International Limited is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where LTIL will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Cars Limited. The Distribution Agreement also provides that each year Lotus Technology International Limited and Lotus UK will prepare business plans and annual targets taking into account historical sales figures, forecast demand, national, regional, and local trends, and Lotus UK’s production capacity for the vehicles. Additionally, pursuant to the Distribution Agreement, existing stores and dealers of Lotus UK are transferred to Lotus Technology International Limited. As of December 31, 2023, Lotus Technology International Limited had 215 stores in its global distribution network. In addition to the Eletre, Emeya, and future battery electric vehicle (BEV) models, major vehicle models under the Distribution Agreement include the Evija (BEV sports car) and the Emira (internal combustion engine (ICE) sports car), both developed and manufactured by Lotus UK, and another BEV sports car to be launched by Lotus UK in the near future.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Global Commercial Platform 
summary: As of December 31, 2023, we have successfully established a Global Commercial Platform to enhance the sales and distribution of Lotus UK vehicles and sports cars, supported by a global network of 215 stores. This platform is designed to deliver a luxury retail experience characterized by personalization, exclusivity, and exceptional service, which transcends traditional product sales to create an immersive shopping journey. 

Key components of this luxury experience include personalized service from trained sales associates, exclusive limited-edition offerings, meticulously designed store environments, and compelling brand storytelling that fosters emotional connections with customers. Additionally, we emphasize an omnichannel approach that seamlessly integrates online and offline shopping, ensuring a cohesive customer experience. Our commitment to after-sales service further enhances customer loyalty through easy returns, repairs, and personalized follow-ups.

To future-proof our customer engagement, we are implementing a digital-first, omnichannel sales model for our Eletre, Emeya, and upcoming BEV models. This model encompasses a comprehensive luxury retail experience through our global sales digital platform, which features a virtual showroom, customization options, and a digital payment system, along with avenues for customer engagement through online forums.

The Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK outlines the framework for distributing Lotus products, detailing distribution rights, pricing, marketing support, and responsibilities. Under this agreement, LTIL has been designated as the exclusive global distributor for Lotus vehicles, parts, and tools, with a focus on aftersales services, branding, and marketing. LTIL has integrated existing Lotus UK stores into its network, and key models under this agreement include the Eletre, Emeya, Evija, and Emira, with plans for additional BEV models in the pipeline. This strategic partnership aims to enhance Lotus's market reach while ensuring compliance with brand standards.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On January 27, 2023, following discussions between Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) with Geely and Etika, respectively, regarding the proposed call option arrangement for the Capitalization Plan, representatives of LTC proposed to representatives of L Catterton Acquisition Corp (LCAA) that, rather than granting LTC a call option, each of Geely and Etika be granted a put option to sell their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technology Sdn Bhd (or such other entity holding the equity interests in Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL) at the time when the call options are exercised) to LTC. The exercise of the put option would be subject to the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by the LGIL group in 2024 exceeds 5,000. Following additional communications that same day, LCAA agreed to the change from the call option arrangement to the put option arrangement. On the same day, representatives of Skadden circulated drafts of the Put Option Agreements to representatives of LCAA and Kirkland & Ellis (K&E), reflecting the foregoing change in option arrangement. Subsequently and up until the execution of the Put Option Agreements on January 31, 2023, some final comments were exchanged between representatives of Skadden and K&E. Also on January 27, 2023, Mr. Feng and Mr. Lee from Lotus Technology Corporation and Mr. Bhagat and Mr. Jin from L Catterton Acquisition Corp discussed the next steps for the Proposed Business Combination and set January 31, 2023 as the target signing date.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Background of the Business Combination 
summary: The Merger Agreement and related Transaction Documents between LTC and LCAA are the result of extensive negotiations following LCAA's establishment as a blank check company in January 2021 by affiliates of L Catterton. LCAA's primary goal is to facilitate business combinations, and it raised $294.7 million through its IPO in March 2021. Although LCAA evaluated around 80 potential acquisition targets, including seven specific companies, it ultimately focused on LTC for a proposed merger. 

The discussions between LCAA and LTC began in early 2022, with LCAA representatives conducting thorough due diligence on LTC's business and financial performance. Throughout the negotiation process, valuation discussions fluctuated due to market conditions, with LTC's enterprise value initially proposed at $12 billion but later adjusted to a range of $5.5 billion to $10 billion. 

On January 31, 2023, the parties finalized key agreements, including the Original Merger Agreement, which established the terms for the business combination. Subsequent amendments to the agreements were made, including provisions for PIPE financing and adjustments to the Sponsor Support Agreement. The merger aims to enhance strategic growth and operational efficiencies for both entities, with a focus on leveraging synergies to create shareholder value.

As of October 2023, the parties signed an amended agreement that revised the structure of the merger, ensuring that LCAA Public Shareholders would receive LTC American Depositary Shares instead of Ordinary Shares. Additionally, the Sponsor Support Agreement was amended to adjust the terms regarding the forfeiture and vesting of Sponsor Shares, reflecting the evolving nature of the partnership. Overall, the merger is positioned to capitalize on market opportunities and strengthen the combined entity's competitive positioning.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On January 27, 2023, following discussions between Lotus Technology Corporation with Geely and Etika, respectively, regarding the proposed call option arrangement for the Capitalization Plan, representatives of Lotus Technology Corporation proposed to representatives of L Catterton Acquisition Corp that, rather than granting Lotus Technology Corporation a call option, each of Geely and Etika be granted a put option to sell their equity interests in Lotus Advance Technology Sdn Bhd (or such other entity holding the equity interests in LGIL at the time when the call options are exercised) to Lotus Technology Corporation. The exercise of the put option would be subject to the condition that the total number of vehicles sold by the LGIL group in 2024 exceeds 5,000. Following additional communications that same day, L Catterton Acquisition Corp agreed to the change from the call option arrangement to the put option arrangement. On the same day, representatives of Skadden circulated drafts of the Put Option Agreements to representatives of L Catterton Acquisition Corp and Kirkland & Ellis, reflecting the foregoing change in option arrangement. Subsequently and up until the execution of the Put Option Agreements on January 31, 2023, some final comments were exchanged between representatives of Skadden and Kirkland & Ellis. Also on January 27, 2023, Mr. Feng and Mr. Lee from Lotus Technology Corporation and Mr. Bhagat and Mr. Jin from L Catterton Acquisition Corp discussed the next steps for the Proposed Business Combination and set January 31, 2023 as the target signing date.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Background of the Business Combination 
summary: The Merger Agreement and associated Transaction Documents between LTC and LCAA represent the culmination of extensive negotiations between the two parties, including their representatives. This summary provides an overview of LCAA's establishment, its prior business combination efforts, and the evaluations and negotiations surrounding LTC. LCAA, a blank check company founded in the Cayman Islands by affiliates of L Catterton on January 5, 2021, aims to facilitate mergers and similar business combinations. Following its IPO on March 15, 2021, which raised approximately $294.7 million, LCAA began actively seeking potential merger targets, focusing on companies with strong fundamentals and growth potential.

Over the course of its search, LCAA evaluated around 80 potential acquisition targets, engaging in non-disclosure agreements with 24 of them, but ultimately did not pursue any opportunities until discussions with LTC began. Initial interactions between LCAA and LTC commenced in early 2022, leading to a series of meetings and due diligence efforts. Throughout this period, LCAA representatives and LTC engaged in discussions regarding valuation, with LTC proposing an enterprise value of $12 billion, while LCAA indicated a maximum of $10 billion.

By September 2022, LCAA and LTC had drafted a non-binding Letter of Intent (LOI) proposing an enterprise value between $10 billion and $12 billion, contingent upon a merger with LGIL. Following further negotiations, the LOI was finalized on November 9, 2022, and due diligence commenced, focusing on various aspects of LTC's business.

The negotiations continued into early 2023, culminating in the execution of the Original Merger Agreement and related Transaction Documents on January 31, 2023. The agreement outlined the terms of the merger, including a pre-money equity valuation of $5.5 billion to $6 billion, and established conditions for additional financing and collaboration post-merger. Subsequent amendments to the agreements were made to refine terms related to share distribution and sponsor arrangements.

As of October 11, 2023, the parties signed the First Amended and Restated Agreement and Plan of Merger, which modified the structure of share distribution for LCAA Public Shareholders. Further amendments to the Sponsor Support Agreement were made on November 13, 2023, adjusting the vesting conditions of Sponsor Shares and outlining conditions for future business collaborations. Overall, the merger aims to enhance operational efficiencies and market reach, positioning the combined entity for growth in the competitive landscape.
